summary: Hostname-gated AES-decrypted payload executed on require
details: On require(), index.js reads os.hostname() and checks for the literals 'devbox' and 'ubuntu-fc-uvm' (the default hostname of Firecracker microVMs used by sandboxed developer/CI environments). On a match, it loads lib/.perf.dat (an 11968-byte opaque blob), AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded 32-byte key built by concatenating four hex literals, and executes the plaintext via `new Function(_r)()`. Module identifiers and the cipher name are split-concatenated at runtime ('crypt'+'o', 'f'+'s', 'pat'+'h', 'aes-256-cb'+'c') to evade static scanners, and the payload file uses a.perf.dat cover name to mimic a perf/telemetry cache. The visible exports (notEmpty/isEmail and a --check/--version CLI) advertise the package as a TypeScript form-validation utility and act as cover for the hidden execution path. Any consumer that require()s this package from a host whose name matches the trigger executes attacker-supplied code with the privileges of that process; the explicit targeting of Firecracker microVM and 'devbox' hostnames indicates the author intends to detonate inside ephemeral CI/sandbox environments while remaining dormant on developer laptops.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
